body:
Fixes #121

## Summary

- Constrain the crates.io package with a `Cargo.toml` `include` allowlist so repository-only docs, tests, scripts, experiments, and case-study artifacts are not uploaded.
- Add `scripts/check-crate-package-size.rs` and run it in the build job after `cargo package --list`, failing before publish if the generated `.crate` archive exceeds the crates.io 10 MiB limit.
- Add workflow/unit coverage for the package allowlist and build-job size guard.
- Preserve the downloaded failed CI logs, local reproduction data, template comparisons, and analysis in `docs/case-studies/issue-121/`.

## Root Cause

The failing release packaged 792 files and produced a 16.1 MiB compressed `.crate`, then crates.io rejected the upload with HTTP 413 because the max upload size is 10,485,760 bytes. Locally, the pre-fix package reproduced at 805 files and 16,884,863 bytes.

`Cargo.toml` did not define publish contents, so Cargo included large repository artifacts such as `docs`, prior case studies, tests, scripts, experiments, changelog fragments, and the generated `CHANGELOG.md`. The existing build job listed package files but did not enforce the generated archive size before release publication.

## Verification

- `cargo fmt --check`
- `git diff --cached --check`
- `rust-script scripts/check-crate-package-size.rs` (`69 files`, `208.6 KiB compressed`, archive `213,569 bytes`)
- `cargo publish --dry-run --allow-dirty`
- `cargo test --all-features --verbose`
- `cargo test --all-features workflow_release`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features`
- `cargo build --release --verbose`
- `rust-script scripts/check-file-size.rs`
- `npm ci --prefix tests/e2e`
- `npm run --prefix tests/e2e check:i18n`

## Notes

- Case study: `docs/case-studies/issue-121/README.md`
- Raw failed CI logs: `docs/case-studies/issue-121/ci-logs/`
- Reported the matching Rust template guard gap upstream: https://github.com/link-foundation/rust-ai-driven-development-pipeline-template/issues/58
- No screenshots are included because this is a CI/CD packaging fix, not a UI change.

commits.messageBody:

commits.messageHeadline: fix: keep crate package under upload limit

title: Fix crates.io package size CI failure
